mnemonics: B.A.T.T.L.E. (Bias-Variance Trade-off, Algorithm selection, Training data quality, Theory understanding, Learning curves, Evaluation metrics) - the key perspectives in the ML battle
The 3 D's of ML Issues: Data (quality/quantity), Design (model choice), Deployment (real-world use)
keyPoints: The bias-variance tradeoff is fundamental to understanding model performance and generalization
No Free Lunch Theorem: no single algorithm performs best for all problems
The quality and quantity of training data significantly impacts model performance
commonMistakes: Focusing only on algorithm selection while ignoring data quality issues
Overfitting models to training data without proper validation techniques
Assuming complex models always perform better than simple ones (violating Occam's Razor)
